Leukocyte esterase [Presence] in Urine by Test strip

tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: Renal function is impaired (elevated serum creatinine), and there is significant proteinuria and ketonuria with low urine specific gravity, suggesting possible renal pathology (e.g., nephrotic syndrome or acute kidney injury). Urine is cloudy, brown, and ammoniacal, but no evidence of infection (negative nitrites/leukocytes). HbA1c is low, excluding chronic hyperglycemia. Recommend further nephrological evaluation and assessment for underlying causes of proteinuria and renal impairment.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Acute ST segment elevation myocardial infarction
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
